messages.content:
Security and Related Activities Control Act 1996:
    • Section 12: Term used: A security officer is a person who for remuneration watches, guards or protects any property. 
      
    • Section 26: Possession of batons:

Except as provided in this section, a security officer’s licence does not authorise the holder to be in possession of a baton while engaged in activities authorised by the licence.


    • Section 35: Term used: crowd controller

A crowd controller is a person who in respect of any licensed premises, place of entertainment, or public or private event or function, as part of his or her regular duties, performs for remuneration any function of — (a) controlling or monitoring the behaviour of persons; (b) screening persons seeking entry; or (c) removing persons for behavioural reasons, or any other prescribed function.

Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913:
    • Section 70A: Trespass (the actual offence)

trespass on a place, means — (a) to enter or be in the place without the consent or licence of the owner, occupier or person having control or management of the place; or (b) to remain in the place after being requested by a person in authority to leave the place; or (c) to remain in a part of the place after being requested by a person in authority to leave that part of the place. (2) A person who, without lawful excuse, trespasses on a place commits an offence. Penalty for this subsection: imprisonment for 12 months and a fine of $12 000.


    • Section 70B: Trespasser may be asked for name and address
    • Section 222: Term used: Assault
    • Section 223: Assault is unlawful
    • Section 333: Deprivation of liberty
    • Section 248: Self Defense
    • Section 254: Use of force to prevent entry and remove people from a place
    • Section 260: Excessive force is unlawful

Criminal Investigation Act 2006:
    • Section 24: Prevention of offences and violence
    • Section 25: Citizens arrest (authority to arrest)

Liquor Control Act 1988
    • Section 115: Refusal of entry (drunk/violent/disorderly/indecent behaviour)
    • Section 120: juveniles are permitted on a licenses premisis when accompanied by a resposnible adult
    • Section 121: Licenses premises, offencesas to juveniles
    • Section 126: suspected juveniles, powers as to – proof of age
    • Section 126C: crowd controllers to be authorised when exercising powers of removal

ask me a multiple choice question based on the data above. Specifically ask What a given section of a given piece of legislation refers to. E.g. "Section 115 of the Liquor Control Act 1988 refers to what?". Tell me if the answer is correct or not

Section 35 of the Security and Related Activities Control Act 1996 refers to what?
A. Possession of batons
B. Term used: crowd controller
C. Term used: security officer
D. Trespass

B. Term used: crowd controller

Correct

Security and Related Activities Control Act 1996:
    • Section 12: Term used: A security officer is a person who for remuneration watches, guards or protects any property. 
      
    • Section 26: Possession of batons:

Except as provided in this section, a security officer’s licence does not authorise the holder to be in possession of a baton while engaged in activities authorised by the licence.


    • Section 35: Term used: crowd controller

A crowd controller is a person who in respect of any licensed premises, place of entertainment, or public or private event or function, as part of his or her regular duties, performs for remuneration any function of — (a) controlling or monitoring the behaviour of persons; (b) screening persons seeking entry; or (c) removing persons for behavioural reasons, or any other prescribed function.

Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913:
    • Section 70A: Trespass (the actual offence)

trespass on a place, means — (a) to enter or be in the place without the consent or licence of the owner, occupier or person having control or management of the place; or (b) to remain in the place after being requested by a person in authority to leave the place; or (c) to remain in a part of the place after being requested by a person in authority to leave that part of the place. (2) A person who, without lawful excuse, trespasses on a place commits an offence. Penalty for this subsection: imprisonment for 12 months and a fine of $12 000.


    • Section 70B: Trespasser may be asked for name and address
    • Section 222: Term used: Assault
    • Section 223: Assault is unlawful
    • Section 333: Deprivation of liberty
    • Section 248: Self Defense
    • Section 254: Use of force to prevent entry and remove people from a place
    • Section 260: Excessive force is unlawful

Criminal Investigation Act 2006:
    • Section 24: Prevention of offences and violence
    • Section 25: Citizens arrest (authority to arrest)

Liquor Control Act 1988
    • Section 115: Refusal of entry (drunk/violent/disorderly/indecent behaviour)
    • Section 120: juveniles are permitted on a licenses premisis when accompanied by a resposnible adult
    • Section 121: Licenses premises, offencesas to juveniles
    • Section 126: suspected juveniles, powers as to – proof of age
    • Section 126C: crowd controllers to be authorised when exercising powers of removal

ask me a multiple choice question based on the data above.

What does Section 254 of the Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913 refer to?
A. Juveniles are permitted on licensed premises when accompanied by a responsible adult
B. Refusal of entry (drunk/violent/disorderly/indecent behaviour)
C. Term used: Assault
D. Use of force to prevent entry and remove people from a place

D. Use of force to prevent entry and remove people from a place

Correct
Specifically ask What a given section of a given piece of legislation refers to. E.g. "Section 115 of the Liquor Control Act 1988 refers to what?". DONT TELL ME THE  ANSWER BRO

Understood! Here's the question:

What does Section 12 of the Security and Related Activities Control Act 1996 refer to?
it gives the definition of security officer, right?
Apologies for the confusion, but the correct answer to the question is "the definition of a security officer." Well done!
Specifically ask What a given section of a given piece of legislation refers to. E.g. "Section 115 of the Liquor Control Act 1988 refers to what?". DONT TELL ME THE  ANSWER BRO

Deprivation of liberty
Correct! Section 333 of the Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913 refers to the offense of deprivation of liberty. Well done!

What does Section 126C of the Liquor Control Act 1988 refer to?
Anyone exercising powers of removal must be authorised to do so
Correct! Section 126C of the Liquor Control Act 1988 refers to the requirement that crowd controllers must be authorized when exercising powers of removal. Well done!

If defines what assault is

Apologies for the confusion. Here's the question:

What does Section 25 of the Criminal Investigation Act 2006 refer to?
citizens arrest
Correct! Section 25 of the Criminal Investigation Act 2006 refers to the authority for citizens' arrest. Well done!
ask another

Certainly! Here's the next question:

What does Section 120 of the Liquor Control Act 1988 refer to?
It discusses the penalties given for supplying a minor with alcohol

Apologies, that is not the correct answer. The correct answer is:

Section 120 of the Liquor Control Act 1988 refers to the permission for juveniles to be on licensed premises when accompanied by a responsible adult.
ask another

Apologies for the incorrect answer. Here's the next question:

What does Section 248 of the Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913 refer to?
self defence
Correct! Section 248 of the Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913 refers to the concept of self-defense. Well done!
ask another

Certainly! Here's the next question:

What does Section 223 of the Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913 refer to?
it declares assault to be unlawful

Apologies, that is not the correct answer. The correct answer is:

Section 223 of the Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913 refers to assault causing bodily harm.
ask another

Apologies for the incorrect answer earlier. Here's the next question:

What does Section 70B of the Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913 refer to?
someone who has committed trespass must provide ID if asked to do so by the property owner. If they dont they facea fine

Apologies for the previous incorrect answer. The correct answer is:

Section 70B of the Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913 refers to the provision that allows a trespasser to be asked for their name and address by the property owner or person in authority. If the trespasser fails to provide this information, they may face a fine. Well done!
ask another

Certainly! Here's the next question:

What does Section 126 of the Liquor Control Act 1988 refer to?
anyone on a licensed premises who is suspected to be a juvenile may be asked for identification

Apologies for the misunderstanding. The correct answer is:

Section 126 of the Liquor Control Act 1988 refers to the powers regarding suspected juveniles, specifically the ability to ask for proof of age. Well done!
ask another

Certainly! Here's the next question:

What does Section 24 of the Criminal Investigation Act 2006 refer to?
it  states that people have the right to use reasonable force to stop a crime

Apologies for the incorrect answer. The correct answer is:

Section 24 of the Criminal Investigation Act 2006 refers to the prevention of offenses and violence.
aks another

Certainly! Here's the next question:

What does Section 26 of the Security and Related Activities Control Act 1996 refer to?
citizens arrest does not give the right to search the premises of a person or their vehicle

Apologies for the confusion. The correct answer is:

Section 26 of the Security and Related Activities Control Act 1996 refers to the possession of batons by a security officer.
ask another

Certainly! Here's the next question:

What does Section 115 of the Liquor Control Act 1988 refer to?
it outlines when it is lawful to remove someone from a premises and includes drunk, disorderly and indecent behaviour

Apologies for the misunderstanding. The correct answer is:

Section 115 of the Liquor Control Act 1988 refers to the refusal of entry based on criteria such as drunk, violent, disorderly, or indecent behavior. Well done!
ask another

Certainly! Here's the next question:

What does Section 254 of the Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913 refer to?
owners of a property are authorised to use reasonable force to remove someone from their property

Apologies for the incorrect answer. The correct answer is:

Section 254 of the Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913 refers to the use of force to prevent entry and remove people from a place.
ask another

Certainly! Here's the next question:

deprivation of liberty

Section 333 of the Criminal Code and Compilation Act 1913 refers to the offense of deprivation of liberty.
